docs.title: Home-Utils
docs.text:
home-utils is a library containing python objects for general development. All of these are intended to be used with debugging purposes.

To install the package, use the GitHub link: 

pip install git+https://github.com/Jtachan/HomeScripts

home-utils does not need of any other third-party Python package for its installation.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
Use the name home-utils to import the functionality of the package:

from home-utils.decorators import *


@time_execution
def heavy_computation(n: int) -> list[int]:
    return [i**2 for i in range(n)]


heavy_computation(50_000)
# Function 'heavy_computation' executed in 0.002569 seconds

Hint

Consider using a logger for a more custom printing.

docs.title: home_utils.decorators
docs.text: General decorators (alphabetically organized) to be used within python callables.

docs.title: time_execution
docs.text:
time_execution(func: tp.Callable[P, T]) -> tp.Callable[P, T]

A decorator that measures and prints the execution time of a function.

The decorator differs from the timeit module as the module is defined to measure small bits of Python code (allowing multiple repetitions) while the decorator displays the runtime of the single decorated function.

Examples:

>>> from home_utils.decorators import time_execution
>>> @time_execution
... def heavy_computation(n: int) -> list[int]:
...     return [i**2 for i in range(n)]
>>> heavy_computation(50_000)
Function 'heavy_computation' executed in 0.002569 seconds

docs.title: count_words
docs.text:
count_words(text: str)

Counts the number of words text.

Considering a given text (with any type of characters), the function counts all alphanumeric words and prints the total count. Numbers (only numeric characters) are also considered as words.
